message:
🐛 Fix concurrent update panic with mutex, tests, and logging (#1350)

* 🐛 Fix concurrent update panic and add update mutex

Root cause: rapid clicks on "Update Now" spawned multiple concurrent
goroutines that all tried to broadcast update_progress over the same
WebSocket connection simultaneously, causing gorilla/websocket to
panic with "concurrent write to websocket connection".

Fixes:
- Add atomic guard in TriggerNow() to reject concurrent updates
- Return 409 Conflict from /auto-update/trigger when update in progress
- Add wsMux to BroadcastToClients() to prevent concurrent WS writes
- Add ref-based guard in frontend onClick to prevent rapid double-clicks
- Hide commit list and update button during active update
- Add updateInProgress field to AutoUpdateStatus API response

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Add CI tests, panic recovery, and structured update logging

- Add 6 unit tests for TriggerNow concurrency guard (stress test with
  100 concurrent goroutines, rejection, release-on-completion, panic
  recovery, IsUpdating, StatusIncludesUpdateInProgress)
- Add recover() in update goroutines to prevent panics from leaving
  the updating flag stuck or crashing the process
- Add structured step-by-step logging with timing to executeDeveloperUpdate
  for faster diagnosis of update failures

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -662,7 +662,8 @@ func (w *PredictionWorker) mergePredictions(byProvider map[string][]AIPrediction
 	return result
 }
 
-// BroadcastToClients sends a message to all connected WebSocket clients
+// BroadcastToClients sends a message to all connected WebSocket clients.
+// Uses wsMux to prevent concurrent writes which cause gorilla/websocket to panic.
 func (s *Server) BroadcastToClients(msgType string, payload interface{}) {
 	message := map[string]interface{}{
 		"type":    msgType,
@@ -675,6 +676,9 @@ func (s *Server) BroadcastToClients(msgType string, payload interface{}) {
 		return
 	}
 
+	s.wsMux.Lock()
+	defer s.wsMux.Unlock()
+
 	s.clientsMux.RLock()
 	defer s.clientsMux.RUnlock()

@@ -66,6 +66,7 @@ type Server struct {
 	registry       *Registry
 	clients        map[*websocket.Conn]bool
 	clientsMux     sync.RWMutex
+	wsMux          sync.Mutex // protects concurrent WebSocket writes
 	allowedOrigins []string
 	agentToken     string // Optional shared secret for authentication
 
@@ -1437,7 +1438,11 @@ func (s *Server) handleAutoUpdateTrigger(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
 		Channel string `json:"channel"`
 	}
 	_ = json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body)
-	s.updateChecker.TriggerNow(body.Channel)
+	if !s.updateChecker.TriggerNow(body.Channel) {
+		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusConflict)
+		json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{"success": false, "error": "update already in progress"})
+		return
+	}
 	json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{"success": true, "message": "update check triggered"})
 }
